evidence_quotes: Coinbases Failure to Provide Account Protection I am a Coinbase One subscriber, which Coinbase advertises as providing account protection against unauthorized activity. However, after reporting this security breach to Coinbase, they stated that account holders are responsible for securing their credentials. Despite following all security best practices and promptly reporting the fraud, Coinbase has not refunded my losses.
I request that Coinbase honor its account protection policies, investigate this security breach, and reimburse the funds lost due to this unauthorized access.
I am submitting this complaint to the CFPB to escalate this matter, as Coinbases failure to protect its customers violates consumer protection standards.
parsed_response.rationale: The narrative expresses anger due to Coinbase's failure to uphold its account protection promises and reimburse losses. user_info_evidence_quotes: I received a Cash App debit card in the mail yesterday ( XX/XX/year> ) with my name which I did not apply for.
I believe I have been a victim of identity theft and someone used my information to open the card under my name.

I have messaged Cash App from the initial email they sent with the case number and questions they needed but have not received a response acknowledging whether they were able to close the account and disable the debit card.
I read online that a lot of people were encountering the same thing where they received a Cash App debit card without opening ones themselves and with most not even having used Cash App like myself.

Those same people that have fraudulent accounts and debit cards opened under their name from Cash App also received 1099-K forms from XXXX, the parent of Cash App, most likely because these fraudulently opened accounts act as a Cash App business account and have bad actors transferring money into these accounts as a way of possibly laundering money I suspect.

someone used my information to open the card under my name
I have already filed a police report notifying them of the identity theft incident
This is not an isolated incident and Cash App needs to address this problem of fraud and identity theft that persist with their company

evidence_quotes: I received a text message from Bank of America asking if I attempted to make a purchase for {$170.00} Krogers with my credit card on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX.
He stated my card had been charged with two transactions and that they were calling to verify that they were fraud since they were suspicious.
I told him no, that is not me and must be a scam.
He then told me in order to stop the wire transaction from going through I had 20 minutes to dispute this with a wire reversal.
I asked how I was to be sure this was a bank of America.
I called back Bank of America to see how this could have happened.
I asked if there was anything Bank of America could do to help with the financial hardship while we were waiting for the response.
She assured me that she would file everything right away but that I needed to fill out a document and return it to them as soon as possible to avoid any delay in my case.
I asked if this fraud could be reported to my analyst since it was the same thing that happened before but this time actual charges.
He stated it could have been hacked from an online retailer, but I just got the card last year and only used it a handful of times.
I called back XX/XX/XXXX and spoke to XXXX from the XXXX DE office who stated that my claim was closed, and they were unsuccessful and retrieving the funds from XXXX XXXX.
I asked what that meant exactly, did they get a response from XXXX XXXX, if so, what did they say.
He stated he couldn't tell me any more information other than that.
I told him about all the misinformation I received and how I don't trust Bank of America and feel like I am being lied to.
I asked if XXXX XXXX responded and if not why bank of America was not reaching back out like they said they would multiple times and he stated that is not possible.
I told them about all the misinformation I received and how I don't trust Bank of America and feel like I am being lied to.
He asked if I wanted it via email or mail. I told him how I never got any of the emails they claimed to send so please send it via the mail.
Bank of America made them the SAME PROMISES they made me. Told me the same things which turned out to be all lies.
They suffered the SAME HUGE LOSSES from wire transfers.
Bank of America has 336 cases for fraud from the last 6 months and over 10 of the cases that were public are exactly like my claim.
